evidence: Person missing recommended: jobTitle, sameAs, hasCredential
FAQPage schema has 10 Questions but page shows ~22 FAQ pairs

No TL;DR block (expected

or paragraph starting with 'TL;DR:')
No visible 'Last updated' / 'Last reviewed' / 'Next review' stamp on page (schema dateModified alone isn't enough for users or AI)
Post is 2219 words (pillar) — include a <nav aria-label="Table of contents"> for LLM outline extraction
Author has no sameAs URLs (should link LinkedIn + at least one other profile)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
Only 3/4 human signals present (author+LinkedIn=false, first-party data=false, original visual=true, 3+ citations=true). High AI-content-flag risk
Specific human voice vs committee 4/10 — Reads like an SEO template with tidy tricolons and 'Quick answer' blocks rather than one person talking.

suggestedPatch.target: A realistic 7-day Oahu group itinerary means front-loading logistics (Hanauma Bay and Diamond Head require reservations), rotating real downtime, and segmenting the group for energy levels.
suggestedPatch.before: A realistic 7-day Oahu group itinerary means front-loading logistics (Hanauma Bay and Diamond Head require reservations), rotating real downtime, and segmenting the group for energy levels.
suggestedPatch.after: Book Hanauma Bay and Diamond Head first, build in actual downtime, and accept your group will splinter by energy level—plan for it.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Specific human voice vs committee" scored 4/10: Reads like an SEO template with tidy tricolons and 'Quick answer' blocks rather than one person talking.

evidence: Genuine opinions vs safe claims 6/10 — Has some real takes (Duke's is touristy and fine, WhatsApp is for ranting), enough to clear the bar.
Unexpected phrasings 5/10 — Leans on predictable phrases like 'non-negotiable,' 'crowd-pleaser,' 'worth the alarm.'

suggestedPatch.after: Skip Hanauma Bay and someone in the group will still be sulking about it on Day 6—just book it.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 5/10: Leans on predictable phrases like 'non-negotiable,' 'crowd-pleaser,' 'worth the alarm.'

evidence: Quotable sentences 4/10 — A few near-quotables, but most lines are functional itinerary prose rather than memorable.

suggestedPatch.target: Be the person who confirms beach towels aren’t $30 at ABC Store.
suggestedPatch.before: Be the person who confirms beach towels aren’t $30 at ABC Store.
suggestedPatch.after: Be the friend who checks ABC Store towel prices before the group pays $30 each—this is the job now.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Quotable sentences" scored 4/10: A few near-quotables, but most lines are functional itinerary prose rather than memorable.

evidence: Intro earns attention 5/10 — Opens with a generic hook plus an SEO 'Quick answer' block before the reader has a reason to care.

suggestedPatch.target: The chaos phase of Oahu group trip planning ends here.
suggestedPatch.before: The chaos phase of Oahu group trip planning ends here.
suggestedPatch.after: If your group chat has 180 unread messages and nobody has booked Hanauma Bay yet, this is the plan that actually ships.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Intro earns attention" scored 5/10: Opens with a generic hook plus an SEO 'Quick answer' block before the reader has a reason to care.

evidence: specificity 6/10 — Day 5's lunch block is the one clear lapse into vague geography ('near Kailua Beach') after the post otherwise names specific venues. Every other day anchors recommendations to named restaurants, dollar figures, and distances. This single section drifts into abstraction and should be fixed to match the rest of the post's standard.

suggestedPatch.target: Lunch is grab-and-go near Kailua Beach. Afternoon, return the car and get everyone ready for town.
suggestedPatch.before: Lunch is grab-and-go near Kailua Beach. Afternoon, return the car and get everyone ready for town.
suggestedPatch.after: Lunch: pick up a plate at Kalapawai Market on Kailua Road (under $15/person, outdoor tables, handles groups). Return the rental to Kailua Surf & Sea or Enterprise Kailua by 3 pm to avoid late fees, then rideshare back to Honolulu.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Quality axis "specificity" scored 6/10: Day 5's lunch block is the one clear lapse into vague geography ('near Kailua Beach') after the post otherwise names specific venues. Every other day anchors recommendations to named restaurants, dollar figures, and distances. This single section drifts into abstraction and should be fixed to match the rest of the post's standard.
